summary: Typosquat of CodebuffAI's `codebuff` CLI fetches and executes unverified binaries from a personal GitHub account
details: Package `codebuff-cli` impersonates the legitimate `codebuff` CLI published by CodebuffAI. The README markets the package as the CodebuffAI product, links to `https://codebuff.com` and `github.com/CodebuffAI/codebuff`, and instructs users to run `npm install -g codebuff` — but this package is published under a different name (`codebuff-cli`) and its binary distribution points at a personal account, `github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli`, unrelated to the claimed publisher. When the user runs the installed CLI, `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` queries `api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest`, downloads `codebuff-<platform>-<arch>` into `~/.codebuff/bin/`, chmods it 0755, and execs it with the user's argv. The download URL is unpinned (mutable `latest` tag, not tied to the package version), there is no hash or signature verification, and the GitHub account hosting the binary does not match the claimed CodebuffAI publisher. The package author can therefore push arbitrary native code to every installer at any time, and that code runs each time the user invokes the CLI. The combination of name/branding impersonation and an unpinned, unverified binary feed from a non-publisher account constitutes a typosquat dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
